title: Cyrtochilum hastiferum
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum hastiferum is classified within the Orchidaceae family, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. It belongs to the subfamily Cypripedioideae. Cyrtochilum hastiferum is also classified as a genus within the subtribe Zygopelmatinae. Further classification places it within the tribe Cypripedieae. Cyrtochilum hastiferum's classification also includes its placement in the family Orchidaceae, which is further divided into several subfamilies.

intro: Cyrtochilum hastiferum, also known as the "sword orchid," is a species of orchid native to South America, characterized by its unique sword-shaped leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain relief, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtochilum hastiferum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cyrtochilum hastiferum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
